questions.text: I'm good at handling my money.
I consider myself a social person.
I often buy things on impulse.
I enjoy treating friends and family.
I worry about money even when I have enough.
I prefer experiences over material things.
I set clear financial goals for myself.
I find it hard to say no to social events because of cost.
I feel pressure to keep up with others' lifestyles.
I save money consistently every month.
I often split bills evenly even if I ordered less.
I avoid checking my bank balance because it stresses me.
I research before making big purchases.
I feel guilty after spending money on myself.
I prefer to invest rather than keep cash in savings.
I talk openly about money with friends.
I feel in control of my financial future.

socialite.title: The Socialite
socialite.label: Your Money Personality
socialite.description: They say money doesn't buy happiness, but you're clearly out here testing that theory.
socialite.detail: For the Socialite, money is basically a VIP pass to enjoy the finer things and share that with those around you. You're not afraid to take a few risks to live your best life.

planner.title: The Planner
planner.label: Your Money Personality
planner.description: You've got spreadsheets for your spreadsheets.
planner.detail: For the Planner, every purchase is a calculated decision. You find peace in budgets and security in savings.

newbie.title: The Newbie
newbie.label: Your financial literacy level
newbie.description: You got {score}/3 correct. Like 25% of under-30s, you're just getting started.
newbie.detail: Finance is a skill, not a personality trait, so don't worry. Ready to explore your relationship with money and find your next move?
